seoTitle: How to Build a Custom Slack App From Scratch
metaDescription: Create a Slack app in the developer dashboard, configure bot token scopes, and install it to your workspace to test live events.
intro: Building custom bots and automations directly into your team's Slack workspace starts in the developer dashboard. This guide covers creating an app from scratch, assigning permission scopes, and generating a bot token to test your first integration.

steps.heading: Create a new app
steps.body: Go to the Slack API developer dashboard and click 'Create New App' to begin the setup process.

steps.heading: Build from scratch
steps.body: Select 'From Scratch', give your app a name, and choose which development workspace to install it in.

steps.heading: Add OAuth scopes
steps.body: Navigate to OAuth & Permissions, scroll to Bot Token Scopes, and add permissions like chat:write and channels:read.

steps.heading: Install the app to your workspace
steps.body: Scroll to the top and click 'Install to Workspace', then review and approve the permission prompt.

steps.heading: Copy your bot token
steps.body: Copy the generated Bot User OAuth Token and store it securely in your local environment configuration.

faq.question: What's the difference between building from scratch and using a manifest?
faq.answer: A manifest lets you define app configuration in a file for faster setup, while building from scratch means configuring each setting manually through the UI.

faq.question: Do I need to know how to code to build a Slack app?
faq.answer: Basic apps with slash commands can be configured without much code, but full functionality typically requires a backend using the Slack SDK.

faq.question: How do I add my bot to a channel?
faq.answer: Use the /invite command inside the target channel to add your newly installed bot.

faq.question: Can I test my app before making it public?
faq.answer: Yes, apps can be tested privately in a development workspace before requesting distribution approval.
